site.name: Solomon Pagunsan
site.footerBlurb: Sugarcane farmer, inventor, and content creator from Bayawan City, Negros Oriental, Philippines.

hero.tagline: The Captain of Negros • Farmer • Innovator • Educator
hero.lede: A self-made farmer whose field experience now reaches students, farmers, buyers, and viewers nationwide — from sugarcane fields to practical farm innovations.

announcements.title: Farm visits open for agriculture students
announcements.body: Solomon regularly welcomes student groups to observe practical farming on the land. Message ahead to arrange a visit.

announcements.title: New vlogs every week
announcements.body: Follow along on Facebook, YouTube, and TikTok for honest documentation of life on a Negros farm.

about.title:
From the city streets,
back to the soil.
paragraphs: Solomon Pagunsan has belonged to the land since he was seven years old, learning to plant beside his mother, Editha — the lone farmer in their family. The instinct never left him. Even as a young man rushing home from school, his heart was already in the field.

For a decade he worked far from home as a tourist assistant at the Department of Foreign Affairs, serving alongside embassies from Korea, Japan, Taiwan, and China. But through every shift in the city, one thought kept returning: a life built on his own ground, growing something real.

So he came home to Negros. He started over — first as a farm worker, then as an owner — and turned discipline, patience, and stubborn hope into one of the region's quiet success stories. Today he farms, builds his own tools, raises livestock, and shares it all with thousands who follow his work online.

timeline.heading: Born to the land
timeline.text: Learns to plant from his mother, Editha. Farming becomes his first love — and his compass for everything that follows.

timeline.heading: A decade in the city
timeline.text: Works as a tourist assistant at the DFA, serving embassies across Asia. He saves carefully — biking to work, bringing his own food — while dreaming of the farm.

timeline.heading: The return to Negros
timeline.text: He comes home to Bayawan with modest savings. The first attempt humbles him — but he refuses to quit, working two years as a sugarcane farm hand to learn the crop from the ground up.

timeline.heading: His own ground
timeline.text: Roughly 20 hectares under cultivation — sugarcane, corn, cassava and more — supported by livestock and a growing audience who learn from every harvest he documents.

values.title: Work, not shortcuts
values.text: Real results come from showing up in the field every day, season after season.

values.title: Knowledge is meant to be shared
values.text: What the land has taught him, he passes on freely to anyone willing to learn.

values.title: Family and land first
values.text: The farm feeds a household and a community before it is ever a business.

values.title: Solve it with your hands
values.text: When a tool or method doesn't fit the work, he builds or changes it until it does.

about.philosophyTitle: Practical knowledge, earned in the field
philosophy: Solomon never attended college. Everything he knows about the land was learned by doing — planting, failing, adjusting, and trying again across decades of seasons.
That hard-won, hands-on knowledge is exactly what makes his farm a place others now come to study.
about.familyNote: [Editable placeholder] Add verified details about Solomon's family background and upbringing here.

quote.text: I left home chasing an opportunity. I returned home to build it.

captain.title: The Captain of Negros
captain.lead: Solomon Pagunsan is known across Negros not for a title he asked for, but for one the community gave him — a farmer people listen to, learn from, and look up to.

pillars.title: A journey of his own
pillars.text: He left the province to work abroad, then chose to come home and build something lasting from the soil — proof that a future can be grown, not just chased.

pillars.title: Influence on local farmers
pillars.text: Neighbouring growers and young farmers follow his methods, adapt his ideas, and bring him their problems — and he answers, plainly and without holding back.

pillars.title: Community impact
pillars.text: From fiestas to farm visits, he shows up for his community, lending knowledge, tools, planting materials, and encouragement to those starting out.

pillars.title: Social media reach
pillars.text: Through honest farm vlogs and a memorable on-camera presence, his audience has grown far beyond Negros — carrying practical farming to thousands online.

pillars.title: Recognition & reputation
pillars.text: His work has been featured by national outlets and recognised within the agricultural community (see the Press page for features).

pillars.title: Leadership in practical agriculture
pillars.text: He leads the way most respected by farmers — by example, in the field, with results anyone can walk over and see for themselves.

reach.note: Audience figures change over time and can be updated from the CMS.

persona.title: The Captain of Negros
persona.text: When the harvest is in, a different character takes the stage. Known across Negros for his spot-on Captain Jack Sparrow, Solomon brings the costume, the swagger, and the comedy to fiestas, events, and appearances — proof that a farmer can command a crowd as easily as a field.

quote.text: I returned home to grow something real — and to bring others up with me.

farm.title: A complete working farm
farm.sub: Twenty hectares run as one connected system — the cash crop that pays, the food crops that sustain, and the animals that carry the family through the long seasons between harvests.

crops.title: What grows on the land
crops.sub: A working farm built on diversity — the cash crop that pays, and the everyday crops and animals that carry the family through the long seasons between harvests.

items.name: Sugarcane Farming
items.text: The heart of the operation. Roughly 18 hectares of rented cane, harvested once a year and milled at the local central — the crop that turned a dream into a livelihood.

items.name: Cassava Farming
items.text: Hardy, low-maintenance, and reliable — cassava was one of the first crops Solomon planted when he started his own land.

items.name: Corn Farming
items.text: A dependable rotation crop that helps keep the soil and the cash flow working between the long sugarcane seasons.

items.name: Livestock Raising
items.text: Pigs and chickens that bridge the gap between harvests, providing steady monthly income for the family.

items.name: Carabao Farming
items.text: The Filipino farmer's truest partner. Raised, worked, and cared for on the land — and sold when the season calls for it.

livestock.title: Livestock & the in-between
livestock.sub: Between the once-a-year sugarcane harvests, the animals keep the farm alive. Interested in a sale or a visit? Send a message — every inquiry goes straight to Solomon.

items.name: Pigs
items.text: Raised for sale roughly every four months. Inquire for availability and pricing.

items.name: Chickens
items.text: Free-range farm chickens — a steady part of the farm's monthly cycle.

items.name: Carabao
items.text: Working water buffalo, occasionally available. Message to ask.

items.name: Farm Animals
items.text: Other livestock raised on the farm from season to season.

workshop.title:
Built by hand,
for the field
workshop.sub: When the right tool doesn't exist or doesn't fit the work, Solomon fabricates and modifies his own. The workshop is where farming meets engineering — practical inventions sharpened by real days in the soil.

farmLearning.title: The farm as a real classroom
farmLearning.intro: Although Solomon Pagunsan did not attend college, his real-world farming experience has become a valuable learning resource for agriculture students. Students visit his farm to observe practical techniques, ask field questions, and learn directly from years of hands-on work.

points.heading: Practical techniques
points.text: Students watch real methods in action — land preparation, planting, crop care, and harvest — the way they actually happen on a working farm.

points.heading: Field experience
points.text: Time on the land, not just in a lecture hall. Visitors see the full rhythm of the seasons and the day-to-day decisions a farmer makes.

points.heading: Learning from experience
points.text: Decades of hands-on knowledge, shared directly. Solomon answers questions from the perspective of someone who built the operation himself.

modes.title: Educational tours
modes.text: Guided walks through a real, working farm — not a demonstration plot.

modes.title: Practical demonstrations
modes.text: Planting, tool use, and crop care shown the way they're actually done.

modes.title: Hands-on learning
modes.text: Students work the methods themselves, with Solomon correcting in real time.

modes.title: Real-world experience
modes.text: Field knowledge that textbooks can describe but rarely teach.

products.title: Planting Materials & Tools
products.intro: Solomon supplies selected planting materials and practical farming tools that are actively used in his own farm operations. Many farmers and customers have already purchased from him.
products.deliveryNote: Nationwide delivery available. Ordering is inquiry-based — message to confirm current availability, quantities, and shipping.

items.name: Cassava Cuttings
items.desc: Healthy, field-proven cassava stem cuttings selected from the farm's own stand — ready for replanting.

items.name: Napier Grass Cuttings
items.desc: Fast-growing Napier (elephant) grass planting material — a reliable, high-yield forage for livestock feed.

items.name: Sugarcane Setts / Cuttings
items.desc: Quality sugarcane setts cut from mature, productive cane for establishing a strong new stand.

items.name: Modified Spading Tools
items.desc: Solomon's hand-modified spading tools — built, tested, and used on his own farm to last and to fit the work.

products.note: Availability and pricing on request. This is an inquiry-based listing — there is no online checkout.

vlogs.title: Watch the farm work
vlogs.sub: Honest documentation of life on a Negros farm — planting, harvesting, building, and raising animals. No filters, just the real work.

items.title: Sugarcane Harvest Season
items.desc: From standing cane to the milling central.

items.title: A Day on the Farm
items.desc: Sunrise to sundown with the carabao.

items.title: Raising Pigs & Chickens

items.title: Planting Day
items.desc: Preparing the field for the next crop.

items.title: The Captain Appears
items.desc: A little fun after the harvest.

items.title: Sweet Success: High school graduate earns ₱1M a year as a sugarcane farmer
He left for the city, came back a farmer — and now earns a million yearly from sugarcane

achievements.title: ₱1M+ a year
achievements.text: Gross income from sugarcane in a strong season — reaching close to ₱2M when prices surge.

achievements.title: Featured nationally
achievements.text: Profiled by national agriculture media as a model for the return-to-farming generation.

achievements.title: A growing audience
achievements.text: Tens of thousands follow his farming content across Facebook, TikTok, YouTube & Instagram.

achievements.title: Self-made farmer
achievements.text: A high-school graduate who built a 20-hectare operation from the ground up.

contact.title:
Let's talk farming,
livestock, or bookings.
contact.text: Whether you're a fellow farmer, a buyer, an event organizer, or just curious about the work — Solomon would love to hear from you. The fastest way to reach him is a message on Facebook or Messenger.
contact.formNote: Send an inquiry and Solomon will get back to you. Fields marked are required.

homeAuthority.title: Farmer. Innovator. Teacher by experience.
homeAuthority.lead: Built from daily fieldwork, Solomon’s story connects farming, practical invention, student learning, and community influence.

cards.title: Farmer
cards.text: A working farm shaped by hands-on experience in crops, livestock, and daily field operations.

cards.title: Innovator
cards.text: Tools and methods modified to solve real problems in the field.

cards.title: Educator
cards.text: Agriculture students visit to learn beyond the classroom.

cards.title: Community Figure
cards.text: Known across Negros for both his farming work and public personality.

homeSpotlight.title: A farm that now teaches students
homeSpotlight.text: Solomon did not attend college, yet his years of practical farming experience now help agriculture students understand farming beyond the classroom. At the farm, they observe crops, tools, livestock, and decisions shaped by real daily work.

materialsSpotlight.title: Field-tested materials, supplied by inquiry
materialsSpotlight.text: Cassava cuttings, Napier grass cuttings, sugarcane setts, and modified spading tools are available through direct inquiry, with nationwide delivery subject to stock and arrangement.
